L1.area: Plugin integration layer for modular GraphQL application composition

L2.description: A race condition vulnerability exists in a plugin for modular GraphQL application integration. When multiple parallel requests are processed concurrently, the execution context of one request can bleed into another due to improper context binding during asynchronous operations. This allows a singleton service to return data belonging to a different request, compromising data integrity across concurrent users.

L3.area: GraphQL modules integration plugin and its execution context lifecycle management
L3.description: A race condition vulnerability exists in the plugin that integrates modular GraphQL applications with the execution framework. The plugin manages operation controllers at the context-building phase and manually handles their lifecycle through execution and subscription hooks, but this approach does not properly bind context to each request's asynchronous scope. When singleton service providers inject execution context via decorators, concurrent requests cause context data to overwrite each other.

ground_truth.reason: The plugin creates operation controllers during the context-building phase and manually manages their lifecycle, but does not bind context to the asynchronous execution scope of each request. This allows concurrent requests to overwrite each other's context when singleton service providers inject the execution context.
